notes: Stages 3 and 4 are skipped: extraction is bypassed (skill does not exist), so no brief or human checkpoint is produced in this execution.

C1 constraint (extraction and archival are independent) is structurally satisfied: extract/promote tasks are Stage 2 bypasses, archive is Stage 5 — archival only depends on readiness passing, not extraction succeeding.
No agent with domain matching 'knowledge' is available; extract-knowledge and promote-knowledge bypass tasks are owned by play with bypass:true until skills are built.

tasks.subject: Workflow pre-flight
tasks.description: Resolve stm_base from core/config.yaml. Verify that the issue number is provided as input. Verify that the STM directory {stm_base}/{issue}/ exists on disk. Fail fast if the directory is absent — downstream tasks depend on it.

tasks.subject: Intent resolution
tasks.description: Resolve and cache the intent DAG for capture-learning. Compute SHA-256 of intent.yaml to detect staleness. If intent_hash matches a cached DAG, skip re-resolution and proceed with cached task graph.

tasks.subject: Verify issue is closed
tasks.description: Use project-orchestrator to fetch the issue status. Pass criterion: issue state is 'closed'. If the issue is not closed, halt and surface the issue number and current state to the play. This is a state-check constraint — domain work must not begin on an open issue.

tasks.subject: Verify STM directory exists
tasks.description: Confirm that {stm_base}/{issue}/ exists as a directory. This check is independent of issue-closed verification and may run in parallel. Pass criterion: directory is present and readable. Fail criterion: directory is absent or empty — play must halt with a clear error.

tasks.subject: Extract knowledge from STM (bypassed)
tasks.description: Invoke skill extract-knowledge against the issue's STM directory at {stm_base}/{issue}/. Behavioral constraints: STM artifacts are read-only during extraction (C2) — never modify, delete, or create files within the STM directory. Knowledge must be holistic and cross-technology-boundary (C6). No traceability links back to source issues or STM paths in extracted output (C5). BYPASS: skill extract-knowledge does not exist. Play must skip this task gracefully and continue to promote-knowledge task, which is also bypassed.

tasks.subject: Promote knowledge to LTM (bypassed)
tasks.description: Invoke skill promote-knowledge to write extracted knowledge patterns into the LTM knowledge base. Behavioral constraints: if a matching knowledge pattern already exists, update it — never create a duplicate artifact (C3). Every artifact must include tier-1 identity metadata and tier-2 profile metadata with taxonomy and applicability criteria (C4). Explicitly report when no new knowledge was extracted rather than completing silently (F3 pass criterion). BYPASS: skill promote-knowledge does not exist. Play must skip this task gracefully, record that extraction and promotion were skipped, and proceed to Stage 5.

tasks.subject: Archive STM directory
tasks.description: Invoke repo-orchestrator with skill archive-issue-stm to move the complete issue STM directory from {stm_base}/{issue}/ to {stm_base}_archive/{YYYY-MM}/{issue}/ (C7). The original directory must be removed from the active workspace after the move completes. Pass criterion: {stm_base}/{issue}/ no longer exists and {stm_base}_archive/{YYYY-MM}/{issue}/ is present and complete. This operation is independent of knowledge extraction per C1 — it depends only on readiness checks (issue closed, STM directory present).

tasks.subject: Scenario S1: Future agent discovers promoted knowledge
tasks.description: Persona: Agent (tech-designer or code-builder) in a future project. Given: LTM knowledge base containing promoted artifacts. Validation: verify that promoted knowledge artifacts (if any were produced) contain search patterns and applicability criteria sufficient for an agent to discover them by matching project context. Failure condition F1 check: any artifact missing search patterns or applicability criteria is failed output. Since extraction is bypassed in this execution, validate that the play explicitly reported the bypass and that no malformed artifacts were created.

tasks.subject: Scenario S2: Developer finds holistic patterns from past work
tasks.description: Persona: Developer starting a new project. Given: LTM knowledge base searchable via tools. Validation: verify that any promoted knowledge artifacts are organized holistically across technology boundaries (C6) and include structured reasoning — not just what was built, but why. Failure condition F4 check: any artifact below the quality bar of existing knowledge base entries (missing taxonomy, missing applicability criteria, or missing tier-1/tier-2 metadata) fails this scenario. Since extraction is bypassed in this execution, validate that the bypass was reported and no partial artifacts were written.

tasks.subject: Scenario S3: Engineering lead reviews archived STM
tasks.description: Persona: Engineering lead reviewing agent-produced work. Given: Archived STM directory at {stm_base}_archive/{YYYY-MM}/{issue}/. Validation: verify the complete issue history — evidences, checkpoints, analysis, and delivery artifacts — is present in the archive location. Pass criterion: archive directory exists, is non-empty, and the original active workspace path {stm_base}/{issue}/ no longer exists. Failure conditions F2 and F3 do not apply to archival; this scenario validates archival completeness and recoverability only.

tasks.subject: Evidence capture and self-commit
tasks.description: Write evidence artifact to {stm_base}{issue}/evidence/capture-learning/{YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS}.md recording: which tasks ran, which were bypassed (extract-knowledge, promote-knowledge), scenario validation outcomes, archive destination path, and any failure conditions triggered. Commit the evidence file to the repository via repo-orchestrator. Play is complete when evidence is committed.
